chunks.text: Psychology is the discipline that studies human behavior and the workings of the mind in a scientific way.
At the end of the nineteenth century
Wilhelm Wundt founded the world's first psychology laboratory at the University of Leipzig.
That moment is regarded as the academic beginning of the field.
the Russian Ivan Pavlov observed that a dog produced saliva when it saw food.
He further discovered that, when a bell was rung repeatedly before the food
the dog began to produce saliva at the sound of the bell alone.
This is the phenomenon known as classical conditioning.
Sigmund Freud argued that our unconscious desire was what drove much of our behavior.
but his great contribution was to spread the concept of " the unconscious " into the world.
In the 1960 s, the experiment of Stanley Milgram showed how ordinary people
under the instruction of an authority figure, could perform cruel actions.
This became one of the most shocking findings of social psychology.
Recent research has revealed how often our decision is bent by many bias.
Though we wish to be rational, we are easily turned aside by emotion and assumption.
Therapy for those who carry anxiety or depression has also been a great contribution of psychology.
The science of the mind is a tool by which we may better understand ourselves.
